g.data-narration: Welcome to More Complex SQL Retrieval Queries. This module covers advanced techniques for sophisticated data analysis.
Basic SQL retrieves simple data, but real-world applications demand advanced techniques for multi-level data analysis.
g.text: Basic SELECT with WHERE retrieves simple data. Complex queries enable:
Multi-level data analysis from multiple tables

g.data-narration: We will explore six key concepts: subqueries, set operations, aggregates, JOINs, EXISTS, and ALL/ANY operators.
A subquery is a nested query. Non-correlated subqueries are independent and execute once, returning a value or set.
g.text: Non-correlated: Independent of outer query, executes once. Returns single value or set.

g.data-narration: Correlated subqueries reference the outer query and execute once per row, checking each row individually.
g.text: References outer query columns. Executes once for each row of outer query.
SELECT dname FROM dept d WHERE EXISTS

g.data-narration: Set operations combine results: UNION all distinct rows, INTERSECT common rows, EXCEPT shows first minus second.
g.text: Combine results from multiple queries with same column count and compatible types.

g.data-narration: Aggregate functions perform calculations: COUNT counts rows, SUM adds values, AVG calculates mean, MAX/MIN find extremes.
g.text: Perform calculations on sets of rows and return single values.

g.data-narration: GROUP BY groups rows for aggregation. WHERE filters rows before grouping, HAVING filters groups after aggregation.
JOINs combine tables: INNER returns matches only, LEFT returns all left plus matches, RIGHT all right plus matches, FULL all rows.
g.text: Combine data from multiple tables based on related columns.

g.data-narration: EXISTS tests if subquery returns any rows. It stops at first match, making it efficient for existence checks.
g.text: Test whether subquery returns any rows. Returns TRUE or FALSE. Efficient - stops at first match.
SELECT dname FROM dept WHERE EXISTS
SELECT dname FROM dept WHERE NOT EXISTS

g.data-narration: ALL returns true if comparison is true for ALL values, equivalent to greater than MAX. ANY returns true for ANY value, equivalent to greater than MIN.
g.text: Compare against all values or any value from subquery results.

g.data-narration: A practical example combining JOIN, GROUP BY, HAVING, and ORDER BY to analyze sales data by product category.
g.text: Integrating multiple concepts: JOIN plus GROUP BY plus HAVING plus ORDER BY
SELECT p.category, SUM(s.qty * s.price) AS total
FROM products p INNER JOIN sales s ON p.id = s.prod_id
GROUP BY p.category HAVING SUM(s.qty * s.price) 100000

g.data-narration: Key exam points: SQL execution order, WHERE versus HAVING, UNION removes duplicates, EXISTS is efficient, ALL equals MAX.
g.text: Practice converting between JOINs and subqueries. Use IS NULL for NULL checks.

g.data-narration: We covered subqueries, set operations, aggregates with GROUP BY and HAVING, JOINs, EXISTS, and ALL and ANY operators for complex SQL retrieval.
g.text: Master these concepts for university exams and real-world database applications!
